title: Post-hospital discharge GP review

medication.text: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.
medication.rationale: Relevant to recording available transfer information and outstanding medicine issues without assuming completeness.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports complete and accurate medicine information, prescribed and non-prescribed medicines, allergies, timing, monitoring, and discussion at care transitions.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

medication.text: Defines reconciliation as identifying an accurate current medicine list, comparing records, recognising discrepancies, documenting changes, and including over-the-counter or complementary medicines.
medication.rationale: Relevant to clinician-performed medicines reconciliation, not the full disease review.
medication.evidence_extract: Defines reconciliation as identifying an accurate current medicine list, comparing records, recognising discrepancies, documenting changes, and including over-the-counter or complementary medicines.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

medication.text: Supports documenting current prescribed, over-the-counter and complementary medicines, changes, discrepancies, discharge information, reconciliation and patient or carer involvement at care transitions.
medication.rationale: Relevant to transfer information and primary-care reconciliation context.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting current prescribed, over-the-counter and complementary medicines, changes, discrepancies, discharge information, reconciliation and patient or carer involvement at care transitions.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.2–1.3.7 — transfer information and medicines reconciliation

medication.text: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.
medication.rationale: Relevant to documenting patient/carer involvement and questions.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting the person’s desired involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-completed discussion of treatment options, benefits and harms without generating a medicine decision.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.5 — involvement, values, preferences, benefits, and harms

sources_used.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Admission diagnosis, course, procedure, complications, pending results, discharge instructions, medicines, current symptoms and follow-up needs are absent.
No stability, test, treatment, referral, readmission or urgency is generated.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
